name: Andorhal
region: Central-western Lordaeron grain hub in the Eastweald breadbasket, reached here by the south feeder running past Strahnbrad

sensoryProfile.sight: Hooded axle lamps, flour dust flashing whenever doors open, narrow side lanes beside counting houses, cooper sheds, rooflines, canvased sacks, and the low practical architecture of a city built to move grain faster than people can think about it.
sensoryProfile.sound: Wagon rattle, mule curses, rope creak, inventory shouts, shutters, then the wrong thinning of ordinary noise once the network starts closing a district around a breach.
sensoryProfile.smell: Grain dust, yeast, vinegar, damp wood, mold in forgotten storage rooms, and the cleaner needle-scent of targeted necromancy slicing through older contamination.
sensoryProfile.touch: Rough plank doors, yeast-room damp, coal-bin grit, cold cellar air, and the bodily pressure of hostile magic moving through walls before the searchers arrive.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Andorhal is no longer merely a busy wrong city. Chapter 8 proves it is a disciplined weaponized city: commercial on the surface, doctrinal underneath, capable of closing around a failure almost before the failure is understood.

establishedFacts.fact: Recent sack marks and ledger notes tie the active grain route southwest through Andorhal.
The village captain understands Andorhal as scale: half the kingdom's flour moving through one place.
For chapter 4, Andorhal is confirmed as the next visible node in the network, not yet as the ultimate source.
Chapter 5 confirms the wider grain web keeps narrowing toward Andorhal through repeating carriers, feeder-road exchanges, and transfer depots rather than one isolated village chain.
Andorhal now reads as a knot in a regional logistics web, not merely the next place-name on a ledger.
The surviving route fragments and scraped tallies make the south feeder road toward Strahnbrad the practical approach line into Andorhal's network.
Barov paperwork appears inside the same road-and-storehouse system that points toward Andorhal, but chapter 5 keeps that as logistics evidence rather than proven family guilt.
Chapter 6 gives the first direct sight of Andorhal as a still-working grain city rather than an off-page destination.
No one on the approach appears to be checking grain loads at the moving gate lines.
The city's surface normalcy is part of the threat: commerce, labor, and distribution continue under the same pressure Jaina can physically feel in the air.
Inside the gates, Andorhal runs on paperwork, seals, inspection rhythm, and grain throughput rather than visible panic.
The city contains parallel investigative lanes: mage-side offices, residue rooms, repack floors, and a granary district full of disguised operatives.
The contamination is introduced during hub repack inside the city rather than only arriving finished from the farms.
At least eight operatives are embedded in the granary district under civilian labor cover.
The west-line lanes and cooper sheds can still hide a clean snatch, which means the city is not omniscient even when it is disciplined.
A compromised witness can be silenced remotely inside the city before physical searchers finish converging.
The response pattern is layered and rehearsed: street teams, roof teams, fallback denial, and district closure all activate within moments of a missed report window.
The map work in the shelter proves Andorhal is the throat of the rollout rather than the destination, with treated grain already moving into northern Lordaeron.
Havel has multiple fallback shelters because the district has felt wrong long enough to require survival infrastructure inside ordinary streets.
By chapter 9, Andorhal functions as the rear hub of the widened Eastweald campaign: the breakthrough behind the team rather than the final destination ahead of it.
Fresh sites north of Andorhal share the same family of binding work found in the city campaign, proving the city opened onto a larger operating landscape rather than an isolated district.

eventsHere.event: Andorhal becomes the next pursuit target once the granary ledgers and shipping marks are read.
The party commits to the south feeder approach after the stripped depot proves the road is being cleaned ahead of them.
The quartet reaches visual confirmation of a living Andorhal and chooses a quiet-fast entry after Arthas refuses to act decisively.
The quartet enters Andorhal under inspection cover and confirms the city is carrying the plague inside ordinary civic rhythm.
Corin opens the mage-side evidence lane while Havel opens the granary-district read, letting the investigation split by competence.
Jaina proves the grain is treated during repack and Sylvanas maps the embedded local cell, leading to a same-night capture plan.
The field team captures a live operative on the west line and proves the local cell can be breached, if only for seconds.
A remote necromantic silencing strike turns the cooper's shed from interrogation site into proof that the city rehearsed witness loss.
The escape through yards, roofs, and fallback shelter proves the partnership can outrun the city even while the city answers with doctrine instead of panic.
Route sheets, city manifests, and local rumor reclassify Andorhal from active hub to distribution throat and attach Kel'Thuzad's name to the work.
The chapter opens after weeks of Eastweald sweeps growing out of the Andorhal breach, which reclassifies the city as the breakthrough behind the action.
